def solve(nums)
  total_x_sum = 0
  n = nums.length
  
  if n <= 1
    return 0
  end
  
  (0...n - 1).each do |i|
    drop = nums[i] - nums[i+1]
    if drop > 0
      total_x_sum += drop
    end
  end
  
  total_x_sum
end

The total sum can exceed standard 32-bit integer limits, so we must use JavaScript's `Number` type which supports up to $2^{53}-1$, or use `BigInt` if the sum exceeds that. Implementation details:
Iterate from $i=0$ to $n-2$.
Calculate $\max(0, nums[i] - nums[i+1])$.
Sum these values.

```javascript
function solve(nums) {
    let total_x_sum = 0;
    const n = nums.length;

    if (n <= 1) {
        return 0;
    }

    for (let i = 0; i < n - 1; i++) {
        const drop = nums[i] - nums[i+1];
        if (drop > 0) {
            // We need to increase nums[i+1] relative to nums[i] by at least 'drop'.
            // This requires starting an operation of size 'drop' at index i+1 
            // (or extending operations started earlier, but since D_min[i+1] - D_min[i] = drop > 0, 
            // a positive difference must be accounted for by new operations starting here).
            total_x_sum += drop;
        }
    }

    return total_x_sum;
}
```
